data.markdown:
# PK Locker: Project Architecture & Data Flow

This diagram visualizes the core architecture of the PK Locker ecosystem, showing the relationship between the Android Management App, the Node.js Backend, and the Device Security protocols.

```mermaid
graph TD
    subgraph "Android Management App (PKlocker)"
        Dashboard[Dashboard Screen]
        Login[Login / Auth]
        Reg[Device Registration]
        EMI[EMI Collection Hub]
        Keys[Key Management]
        
        Dashboard --> Reg
        Dashboard --> EMI
        Dashboard --> Keys
    end

    subgraph "Backend API (Node.js/Express)"
        AuthAPI[Auth Routes]
        DeviceAPI[Device Management]
        EmiAPI[EMI & Penalties]
        KeyAPI[License Key Engine]
        AdminAPI[Admin Dashboard]
        
        AuthAPI --- DB[(MongoDB)]
        DeviceAPI --- DB
        EmiAPI --- DB
        KeyAPI --- DB
    end

    subgraph "Locked Device (Customer)"
        LockService[Security Lock Service]
        StatusCheck[Periodic Status Sync]
        LockService --- StatusCheck
    end

    Login <==> AuthAPI
    Reg <==> DeviceAPI
    EMI <==> EmiAPI
    Keys <==> KeyAPI
    StatusCheck <==> DeviceAPI
```

### Core Business Logic
| Module | Responsibility | Primary Data Model |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Authentication** | Shopkeeper login, registration, and token management. | `Shopkeeper` |
| **Device Hub** | Registering new devices and tracking their online/offline/locked status. | `Device` |
| **EMI Engine** | Calculating due dates, tracking payments, and triggering remote locks. | `EmiPayment` |
| **License Keys** | Inventory management for Android/iOS activation licenses. | `Key` & `KeyOrder` |

### Payment Verification Flow (Manual)
1. **Shopkeeper** requests keys via App.
2. App shows **Business Till ID / IBAN**.
3. Shopkeeper uploads **Payment Screenshot**.
4. **Admin** approves request in Backend.
5. Keys are allocated to Shopkeeper's quota.
data.title: Project Architecture Visualized
